title: Scabies contact documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting close skin contact, household context, exposure before symptoms, characteristic distribution, and clinician-completed visual, dermoscopic, or microscopy assessment without inferring diagnosis.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact WHO transmission and diagnosis section supports documenting a scabies-contact context, onset, relevant contact and household history, skin inspection and distribution without asserting infestation.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting close skin contact, household context, exposure before symptoms, characteristic distribution, and clinician-completed visual, dermoscopic, or microscopy assessment without inferring diagnosis.

presenting_history.text: Supports documenting itch, night worsening, burrows, papules, site distribution, infant or child distribution, immunosuppression, crusting, and secondary skin infection context.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact symptom and population section supports recording itch, burrows, papules, pediatric distribution, immunosuppression, crusting and secondary infection only when assessed.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports documenting itch, night worsening, burrows, papules, site distribution, infant or child distribution, immunosuppression, crusting, and secondary skin infection context.

additions.text: Supports documenting close skin contact, household context, exposure before symptoms, characteristic distribution, and clinician-completed visual, dermoscopic, or microscopy assessment without inferring diagnosis.

source_limitations: Exposure date and type, index case status, symptoms and distribution, household and institutional contacts, pregnancy and age, immune status, examination, diagnosis, microscopy or dermoscopy, medicine, contact management, infection control, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
